questions.question: Define pass-by-value and pass-by-reference in the context of Java programming. Explain how these concepts apply to primitive and reference types.
keyPoints: Pass-by-value: a method of passing arguments where a copy of the original value is passed to the method.
Pass-by-reference: a method of passing arguments where a reference to the original value is passed to the method.
Primitive types are passed by value, while reference types are passed by value of the reference.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the behavior of primitive and reference types when passed as arguments to a method in Java. Use code examples to illustrate the differences.
keyPoints: Primitive types are passed by value, meaning changes to the argument within the method do not affect the original value.
Reference types are passed by value of the reference, meaning changes to the state of the object within the method affect the original object.
Code examples demonstrate the differences in behavior between primitive and reference types.

questions.question: Explain briefly why a swap method for two primitive integers does not work as intended in Java.
keyPoints: The swap method receives copies of the original values, which are then swapped within the method.
The changes do not affect the original variables, which remain unchanged after the method returns.

questions.question: Discuss the implications of Java's pass-by-value mechanism on the behavior of methods that receive arrays and other reference types as arguments. Use a complex scenario to illustrate the effects of modifying array elements, reassigning array references, and modifying primitive integers within a method.
keyPoints: Java's pass-by-value mechanism means that methods receive copies of the original values, including array references.
Modifying array elements within a method affects the original array, while reassigning the array reference only affects the local copy.
Modifying primitive integers within a method only affects the local copy, leaving the original variable unchanged.
